task: v0.4.21 — HTTP+SSE bridge + portable HTML frontend (cross-system portable)
summary: v0.4.21 — HTTP+SSE bridge + portable HTML frontend (cross-system portable)
reason:
Chairman directive: '除了这个win系统的前端，给html的也补充一下，方便移植别的系统.'

Two parts:

A) HTTP+SSE bridge (new file crates/pipe-server/src/ws_bridge.rs):
   The pipe server's primary transport is named pipes
   (Windows) / Unix domain sockets (macOS/Linux). That's the
   right choice for the Tauri desktop shell on the same machine
   — but it locks out every browser, on any OS.

   The bridge exposes the same JSON-RPC API + events stream
   over loopback HTTP:
     POST /rpc      — JSON-RPC 2.0 (Content-Length body)
     GET  /events   — Server-Sent Events
     GET  /health   — health probe
     OPTIONS *      — CORS preflight

   - bind to 127.0.0.1:8765 by default (FLOWNTIER_HTTP_BRIDGE env var to override)
   - refuses non-loopback peers (defence-in-depth even if bind is widened)
   - raw HTTP/1.1 implementation; no new dependencies (tokio + tracing already in)
   - spawned by bin/flowntier-runtime.rs after register_all, alongside the quota scheduler
   - /events is a real SSE stream: `data: <json>\n\n` per AgentEvent, browsers auto-reconnect
   - public API: run_http_bridge(bind, dispatcher, events_tx) and run_http_bridge_on(listener, ...) for tests
   - bind_listener('127.0.0.1:0') lets tests learn the OS-assigned port without rebind race

B) Portable HTML frontend (new dir apps/html-frontend/):
   Single index.html + app.js (~400 lines vanilla JS) + README.md.
   Talks to the bridge directly via fetch() + EventSource. No
   Tauri, no bundler, no framework. Runs from any browser, on
   any OS.

   Features shipped in this commit:
   - language picker (zh-CN / en-US, persisted in localStorage)
   - role picker (chief / worker / planner / critic:a / critic:b / reporter)
   - resolve status line under the role picker (default / no key / unconfigured)
   - chat input + transcript (renders text deltas + tool events + done)
   - quota status side panel (polled every 15s; renders failed / pending_5h_wait / rate_limited states)
   - reset button on rate_limited rows (POST /api/quota/reset)
   - quota nudge banner above the chat (auto-renders when scheduler emits QUOTA_NUDGE: events)
   - log pane (collapsed) for debugging

   Deferred (v0.5 follow-up):
   - Settings pane (provider management, secret editing, role assignments)
     — endpoints already exist; UI wrapping is straightforward but
     not in this commit's scope.

C) Tests (5 new e2e tests in crates/pipe-server/tests/e2e_pipe.rs):
   - http_bridge_health_returns_ok
   - http_bridge_rpc_round_trips  (POST /rpc with JSON-RPC body → JSON-RPC response)
   - http_bridge_rpc_missing_content_length_returns_400
   - http_bridge_cors_preflight_returns_204
   - http_bridge_unknown_route_returns_404
   All 5 pass. Workspace tests: 159 passed (30 in pipe-server + the rest).

D) Build / commit:
   - cargo test -p pipe-server → 30/30 passed (5 new bridge + 25 pre-existing)
   - No new NSIS ship needed (no UI changes for the Tauri shell)
   - No runtime rebuild needed (bridge is part of pipe-server binary; the new runtime binary will be picked up by the v0.4.20 → v0.4.21 rebuild when chairman does the next hand-test)

Honest risk callouts:
- /events SSE stream never closes naturally; we half-close after each
  handler returns but the connection is meant to be long-lived.
  EventSource auto-reconnects on TCP close. Tested manually.
- The HTML frontend intentionally bypasses the Tauri IPC layer
  (no shell). It works only when the runtime's HTTP bridge is
  bound (default 127.0.0.1:8765). If chairman restarts the runtime
  with a non-loopback bind, the browser will refuse.
- bridge handshake code is ~400 lines hand-rolled (vs. pulling in
  hyper/axum). Robust to the test cases but not stress-tested
  against malformed chunked transfer, HTTPS upgrade, etc. — out of
  scope for v0.4.21.

Deferred from v0.4.20 still tracked (next round):
- per-provider HTTP error-code parsing (Anthropic 529 / OpenAI 429 / etc.)
- textarea overlay bug investigate (v0.4.19 residual)
- Settings provider-management UI in the HTML frontend
